name: Sierra de Nogueruelas and Ermita de Santa Isabel Loop
name_original: Sierra de Nogueruelas y Ermita de Santa Isabel — circular desde Rubielos de Mora
description: This moderate 27.6-mile mountain biking route from Rubielos de Mora offers scenic views and passes the historic Ermita de Santa Isabel.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rubielos De Mora offers diverse terrain across the Maestrazgo Mountain Range, characterized by impressive gorges carved by the Mijares and Rubielos Rivers. The region features extensive pine forests and wide pastures, providing varied backdrops for rides. Riders can explore trails that range from river valleys to mountainous ascents, with elevations offering panoramic views over the Teruel Plateau. Best mountain bike trails around Rubielos De Mora The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Nogueruelas and Ermita de Santa Isabel Loop, a 27.6 miles (44.4 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route traverses mountain ranges and forests, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Carlos IV Bridge – Olba Village loop from Rubielos de Mora, a moderate 20.5 miles (33.1 km) path. This trail follows river channels and passes through scenic villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente de la Salud – Olba Village loop from La Verdeja, a 20.5 miles (33.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and past natural springs, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Rubielos De Mora is defined by river gorges, mountain ranges, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Rubielos De Mora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rubielos De Mora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Rubielos de Mora?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike routes in the Rubielos de Mora area. This includes a diverse range of trails, with 6 easy, 55 moderate, and 19 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Rubielos de Mora?
A: The terrain around Rubielos de Mora is incredibly varied. You'll find trails traversing the majestic Maestrazgo Mountain Range, carving through impressive gorges alongside the Mijares and Rubielos Rivers, and winding through extensive pine forests and wide pastures. Expect a mix of river valleys, mountainous ascents, and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Rubielos de Mora?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. The region offers 6 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails typically feature less challenging gradients and smoother surfaces.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Rubielos de Mora?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails around Rubielos de Mora are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40374113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos IV Bridge – Olba Village loop from Rubielos de Mora&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33876314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Salud – Olba Village loop from La Verdeja&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer access to numerous natural and historical landmarks. You can encounter impressive gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, natural springs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Salud&lt;/a&gt;, and unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1756156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red earth&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba&lt;/a&gt; and historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos IV Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenges. There are 19 difficult mountain bike trails in the area, featuring significant elevation changes and technical sections. For a demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rubielos de Mora – Olba Village loop from Rubielos de Mora&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 81 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Rubielos de Mora?
A: The mountain bike trails in Rubielos de Mora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the river gorges and forests, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels. The well-preserved historical towns and villages along the way also add to the appeal.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options near Rubielos de Mora?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 6 easy routes available are generally suitable for families with some biking experience. These trails offer gentler gradients and less technical terrain, allowing for a more relaxed and enjoyable ride together through the scenic landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Rubielos de Mora?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Rubielos de Mora. The weather is typically milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if visiting during this season. Winters can be cold, and some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow.

Q: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or places to rest along the routes?
A: Yes, you can find facilities along some routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2614427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt; is a convenient spot for a break. Additionally, the region's natural springs, like Fuente de la Salud, offer refreshing stops. Many routes also pass through charming villages where you can find amenities.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in this area?
A: Trail distances vary significantly, from shorter loops to longer, more challenging expeditions. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38253129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Salud – Olba Village loop from Jardin de las musas&lt;/a&gt; is about 21.8 km (13.5 miles) and takes around 2 hours. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005029985/sierra-de-nogueruelas-and-ermita-de-santa-isabel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Nogueruelas and Ermita de Santa Isabel Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be over 44 km (27.6 miles) and take more than 4 hours to complete.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra de Nogueruelas y Ermita de Santa Isabel mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This moderate mountain biking route usually takes around 4 hours and 22 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 44.4-kilometer circular trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate (STS S2) and features diverse terrain. It initially follows a broad track (pistón) up to the Hermitage of Santa Isabel. While specific details for every section are limited, mountain biking in the wider Sierra de Gúdar region often includes forest tracks, singletracks, and rocky sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the Hermitage of Santa Isabel, which also features a forest surveillance post and archaeological excavations of two Arab wells. Other highlights include the Stream of the Camino de Mora and the Mas de Chupadillas viewpoint. The starting point, Rubielos de Mora, is also a beautiful town with medieval architecture.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several established paths. You'll find parts of the GR-160, the Camino del Cid (specifically 'La Conquista de Valencia E05' and 'La Conquista de Valencia'), PRTE 21, Camino al Alto de la Venta, and Senda Peñarroya.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Nogueruelas y Ermita de Santa Isabel trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate and is best suited for riders with good physical fitness and cycling experience. It aligns with an STS S2 difficulty level, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, the Gúdar-Javalambre region generally experiences pleasant weather for outdoor activities during spring and autumn. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations. Spring and autumn typically offer comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Rubielos de Mora?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Rubielos de Mora. As a historical town, there are typically public parking areas available within or on the outskirts of the town. It's advisable to check local signage for designated parking spots upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sierra de Nogueruelas y Ermita de Santa Isabel trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas like the Sierra de Nogueruelas. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please practice responsible pet ownership and clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sierra de Nogueruelas y Ermita de Santa Isabel trail. It is generally open to the public for recreational use.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers impressive views, especially from the Hermitage of Santa Isabel, which sits at an altitude of 1,175 meters. The wider Gúdar-Javalambre region, which the trail traverses, is known for its picturesque landscapes, including charming villages, ancient farmhouses, rivers, springs, and distinctive forests.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Rubielos de Mora?
faqs.answer: Rubielos de Mora is a well-known historical town, so you can expect to find various amenities. It offers cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it a convenient base for starting and finishing your mountain biking adventure.
